title: Inline Print
description: Use the end parameter to print "Counting: 1 2 3 4 5" all on the same line. Use multiple print() calls.
starterCode:
# Print numbers 1-5 on the same line
# Use print() with end parameter
# Output: Counting: 1 2 3 4 5
solution:
print("Counting:", end=" ")
print(1, end=" ")
print(2, end=" ")
print(3, end=" ")
print(4, end=" ")
print(5)

title: Refactor: Organize This Mess
description: The code below prints an introduction but is messy. Refactor it to use blank lines, comments, and consistent formatting. Do not change the output — just make the code cleaner.
starterCode:
# This code works but is messy — refactor it
print("=== My Profile ===")
print("Name: Jane")
print("Age: 30")
print("---")
print("Hobbies:")
print("- Reading")
print("- Painting")
print("---")
print("""The end""")
solution:
# My Profile — Introduction Card
print("=== My Profile ===")
print("Name: Jane")
print("Age: 30")
print("---")
print("Hobbies:")
print("- Reading")
print("- Painting")
print("---")
print("The end")

title: Combined Features Challenge
description: Write a program that uses at least 3 different print() features (sep, end, string multiplication, newline, multiple arguments) to create a formatted receipt for a store purchase. Include item names, prices, a line total, and a decorative border.
starterCode:
# Create a store receipt using print() features
# Use: border with *, items with sep, 
# multiple arguments, end for alignment
solution:
print("*" * 30)
print("      PYTHON STORE")
print("*" * 30)
print("Item", "Price", sep="    ")
print("-" * 30)
print("Apples", "$3.00", sep="   ")
print("Bread", "$2.50", sep="    ")
print("Milk", "$4.00", sep="     ")
print("Eggs", "$5.50", sep="     ")
print("-" * 30)
print("Total:", end=" ")
print("$15.00")
print("*" * 30)

title: Create Your Own Challenge
description: Create a Python program that produces a creative output of your choice. It must use at least 5 print() statements and incorporate at least 2 different print() features (sep, end, string multiplication, or newline). The output must be something visually interesting or useful.
starterCode:
# Create your own creative output
# Use at least 5 print() statements
# Use at least 2 print() features
solution:
# Calendar header example
print("=" * 35)
print("     JUNE 2026")
print("=" * 35)
print("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun", sep="  ")
print("-" * 35)
print(" 1", " 2", " 3", " 4", " 5", " 6", " 7", sep="   ")
print(" 8", " 9", "10", "11", "12", "13", "14", sep="  ")
print("=" * 35)
